Overview
Jobs
Life
About us
Keysight empowers innovators to explore, design, and bring world-changing technologies to life.
As the industry’s premier global innovation partner, Keysight’s software-centric solutions serve engineers across the design and development environment, enabling them to deliver tomorrow’s breakthroughs at speed and with reduced risk.
 
Keysight leverages its strength as the world’s leading test and measurement provider and today enables innovators to push the boundaries of engineering by quickly solving design, emulation, and test challenges to help create the best product experiences.
 
Whether you're looking to improve your design and development process, optimize and secure your network, or harness AI and digital twins to get a head start on technologies like 6G, AV and EV, IoT, or quantum computing — Keysight accelerates innovation across the workflow with intelligent insights built on the most accurate measurements.  Our fusion of technology knowledge, measurement science expertise, and tailored solutions helps you forge ahead with confidence in our connected and dynamic world.
 
Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) is an S&P 500 technology company, headquartered in Santa Rosa, California, with offices and manufacturing worldwide. Keysight owns 2,000+ patents and our ~15,000 employees work with nearly 32,000 customers worldwide to start technology revolutions. Keysight customers span the communications, industrial automation, aerospace and defense, automotive, energy, semiconductor, and general electronics markets.

Join us! To accelerate innovation to connect and secure the world, innovators start here, with Keysight.

We are excited to announce our expanded collaboration with the Midwest Microelectronics Consortium (MMEC). It’s a regional hub in a Department of Defense-sponsored network, the Microelectronics Commons, established under the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act. Startups selected for MMEC's ScaleBridge Labs incubator gain access to Keysight's electronic design automation (EDA) software and e-learning resources. 

Read more:
https://ow.ly/fpVM50Zs0BI

The future of wireless will be defined by more than speed. At Princeton University, Assistant Professor Yasaman Ghasempour is exploring how wave
#physics
, millimeter-wave, and terahertz systems can help networks sense their surroundings, localize users, adapt to blockage, and support AI-driven applications. Her research is helping advance the technologies and architectures that could shape
#6G
. 

Discover how researchers are reimagining what
#wireless
networks can do:
https://ow.ly/5MfG50ZrXMm

AI infrastructure does not scale on GPUs alone. The latest edition of the Keysight Learn newsletter explores how advanced
#silicon
, high-speed
#interconnects
, networking, power delivery, and distributed clusters must work together to deliver performance at scale. Discover practical strategies for validating
#AI
infrastructure from design through deployment under real-world, system-level conditions. Read the newsletter to learn what it takes to build scalable, efficient AI data centers.
Scale AI Infrastructure: From Chip to Cluster

Report this post
Keysight and
Rambus
collaborated to demonstrate that side-channel vulnerabilities in an Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) design can be detected at the pre-silicon stage. Rambus selected a pre-release version of one of its AES designs, in which an SCA vulnerability had previously been identified through traditional Field-Programmable Gate Array-based (FPGA)
#validation
testing. Using Keysight Inspector Pre-Silicon SCA, the Keysight Device Security team identified side-channel leakage from AES design signals directly from simulation, along with its root cause, without needing physical
#hardware
.  The results closely matched traditional FPGA validation performed by the Rambus team. 

Learn more:
https://ow.ly/2U6R50Zrsn3

Semiconductor innovation depends on connected workflows from design through validation and test. 

We’re excited to launch the new Keysight
#Semiconductor
Innovation experience, bringing together solutions that help engineering teams: 

• Design and characterize next-generation devices 
• Validate wafer-level performance with confidence 
• Accelerate photonics IC development and test 

Explore how integrated measurement workflows help improve correlation, reduce development risk, and speed time to insight.
https://ow.ly/Y0pe50ZqS5R

For Professor Josep Miquel Jornet, the next chapter of terahertz
#wireless
is about more than bandwidth. His research at Northeastern University explores near-field beam shaping, resilient links, and communication systems that can adapt to their physical environment. 

Discover how greater control over radio waves could shape future
#6G
networks. Read the full researcher spotlight:
https://ow.ly/mVi150ZqRbp

Today we are excited to announce Keysight Multiphysics, a design and verification solution that addresses the
#physics
interactions driving failure in modern
#electronic
designs. The structural analysis application covering drop, shock, and vibration enables
#engineering
teams to identify and fix problems earlier, before a prototype is built. 

Learn more:
https://ow.ly/HAGY50ZqNOn

Securing modern embedded systems requires a deeper understanding of how devices operate and where attackers can exploit physical and interface-level vulnerabilities. Keysight’s expert-led bootcamps provide practical training in embedded security and sensor measurement using workflows applied across advanced
#electronic
designs. Enroll now to strengthen your hardware
#security
, measurement, and validation skills.

Embedded Systems Security bootcamp:
https://ow.ly/kBHv50Zqbzp
Fundamentals of sensor measurements using a digital
#multimeter
:
https://ow.ly/EWO750Zqbzs
